Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a temporal profile model with several pairs of activities and their average time intervals and standard deviations We are to identify anomalies hypothesize reasons and propose verification SQL queries Step 1 Identify anomalies in the temporal profile model Looking at the provided temporal profile R P 90000 3600 Average 25 hours STDEV 1 hour very low STDEV for a 25 hour average P N 604800 172800 Average 7 days STDEV 2 days long and variable A C 7200 3600 Average 2 hours STDEV 1 hour closing soon after assignment E N 300 60 Average 5 minutes STDEV 1 minute very quick from evaluation to notification Also note that we have an expected process R A E P N C Some pairs are not in the expected sequence but the model includes them We are to look for unusual times

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 2 Generate hypotheses For each anomaly 1 R P Average 25 hours with a very low standard deviation 1 hour This could be due to a fixed policy that approves claims exactly 25 hours after receipt without considering the evaluation step Possibly a system that automatically approves after a fixed time or a batch process that runs every 25 hours 2 P N Average 7 days with a high standard deviation 2 days This could be due to a backlog in the notification system where notifications are sent in batches every few days Some claims requiring additional manual steps before notification leading to variable delays 3 A C Average 2 hours from assignment to close This could be due to some claims being closed immediately after assignment without proper evaluation possibly due to fraudulent or invalid claims that are quickly rejected A quick resolution for certain types of claims that require little evaluation 4 E N Average 5 minutes from evaluation to notification This could be due to an automated notification system that sends notifications immediately after evaluation Or if evaluation is done by the adjuster and they have a habit of immediately notifying this might be too quick and could indicate that the evaluation is not thorough

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For each anomaly 1 R P Average 25 hours with a very low standard deviation 1 hour This could be due to a fixed policy that approves claims exactly 25 hours after receipt without considering the evaluation step Possibly a system that automatically approves after a fixed time or a batch process that runs every 25 hours 2 P N Average 7 days with a high standard deviation 2 days This could be due to a backlog in the notification system where notifications are sent in batches every few days Some claims requiring additional manual steps before notification leading to variable delays 3 A C Average 2 hours from assignment to close This could be due to some claims being closed immediately after assignment without proper evaluation possibly due to fraudulent or invalid claims that are quickly rejected A quick resolution for certain types of claims that require little evaluation 4 E N Average 5 minutes from evaluation to notification This could be due to an automated notification system that sends notifications immediately after evaluation Or if evaluation is done by the adjuster and they have a habit of immediately notifying this might be too quick and could indicate that the evaluation is not thorough

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 3 Propose verification approaches using SQL queries We will write queries to check the actual time differences between these activities in the claim events table Note The claim events table has claim id activity and timestamp We can compute the time between two activities for each claim by joining the table on itself for the same claim id and different activities We will write queries for each of the anomalies we identified

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 1 Compute the average and standard deviation of time between R and P SELECT AVG EXTRACT EPOCH FROM t2 timestamp minus t1 timestamp as avg seconds STDDEV EXTRACT EPOCH FROM t2 timestamp minus t1 timestamp as stdev seconds FROM claim events t1 JOIN claim events t2 ON t1 claim id equals t2 claim id WHERE t1 activity equals R and t2 activity equals P and t1 timestamp less than t2 timestamp If the average is around 90000 and the standard deviation is very low like 3600 seconds then it confirms the anomaly

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 2 Check if the anomaly is present in all claim types or only in some SELECT c claim type AVG EXTRACT EPOCH FROM t2 timestamp minus t1 timestamp as avg seconds STDDEV EXTRACT EPOCH FROM t2 timestamp minus t1 timestamp as stdev seconds FROM claim events t1 JOIN claim events t2 ON t1 claim id equals t2 claim id JOIN claims c ON t1 claim id equals c claim id WHERE t1 activity equals R and t2 activity equals P and t1 timestamp less than t2 timestamp GROUP BY c claim type

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 3 Compute the average and standard deviation of time between P and N and break down by claim type SELECT c claim type AVG EXTRACT EPOCH FROM t2 timestamp minus t1 timestamp as avg seconds STDDEV EXTRACT EPOCH FROM t2 timestamp minus t1 timestamp as stdev seconds FROM claim events t1 JOIN claim events t2 ON t1 claim id equals t2 claim id JOIN claims c ON t1 claim id equals c claim id WHERE t1 activity equals P and t2 activity equals N and t1 timestamp less than t2 timestamp GROUP BY c claim type

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Query 4 Check for claims that are assigned A and then closed C without any evaluation E in between SELECT t1 claim id t1 timestamp as assign time t2 timestamp as close time EXTRACT EPOCH FROM t2 timestamp minus t1 timestamp as time diff seconds FROM claim events t1 JOIN claim events t2 ON t1 claim id equals t2 claim id LEFT JOIN claim events t3 ON t1 claim id equals t3 claim id AND t3 activity equals E AND t3 timestamp BETWEEN t1 timestamp and t2 timestamp WHERE t1 activity equals A and t2 activity equals C and t1 timestamp less than t2 timestamp and t3 event id IS NULL This will find claims that were closed within 2 hours or any time after assignment and had no evaluation event in between

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Query 5 For the A C anomaly check if the adjusters involved are in a particular region or specialization SELECT a region a specialization COUNT as count quick closure FROM claim events t1 JOIN claim events t2 ON t1 claim id equals t2 claim id LEFT JOIN claim events t3 ON t1 claim id equals t3 claim id AND t3 activity equals E AND t3 timestamp BETWEEN t1 timestamp and t2 timestamp JOIN adjusters a ON t1 resource equals a adjuster id WHERE t1 activity equals A and t2 activity equals C and t1 timestamp less than t2 timestamp and EXTRACT EPOCH FROM t2 timestamp minus t1 timestamp less than or equals 7200 and t3 event id IS NULL GROUP BY a region a specialization

Name: Validation - NE
Text: Query 6 Check the claims where the time between E and N is less than 10 minutes and see if they are associated with particular adjusters or claim types SELECT c claim type a adjuster id a region COUNT as count quick notify FROM claim events t1 JOIN claim events t2 ON t1 claim id equals t2 claim id JOIN claims c ON t1 claim id equals c claim id JOIN adjusters a ON t1 resource equals a adjuster id WHERE t1 activity equals E and t2 activity equals N and t1 timestamp less than t2 timestamp and EXTRACT EPOCH FROM t2 timestamp minus t1 timestamp less than or equals 600 GROUP BY c claim type a adjuster id a region

Name: Validation - IND
Text: Query 7 Check if claims that go from E to N quickly also have the P activity and if so in what order SELECT t1 claim id t1 timestamp as evaluate time t2 timestamp as notify time t3 timestamp as approve time t3 activity as approve activity FROM claim events t1 JOIN claim events t2 ON t1 claim id equals t2 claim id LEFT JOIN claim events t3 ON t1 claim id equals t3 claim id AND t3 activity equals P AND t3 timestamp greater than t1 timestamp WHERE t1 activity equals E and t2 activity equals N and t1 timestamp less than t2 timestamp and EXTRACT EPOCH FROM t2 timestamp minus t1 timestamp less than or equals 600 ORDER BY t1 claim id This will show if the approval happened before or after the notification

Name: Validation - IND
Text: We have now proposed several queries to verify the anomalies Lets summarize We identified four anomalies and for each we proposed queries to check the actual data and break down by claim type adjuster etc Note The above queries are written for PostgreSQL and use EXTRACT EPOCH FROM to get seconds We must be cautious of the fact that the same claim might have multiple events of the same type The schema does not specify but typically each activity is recorded once per claim However the query for A C with a left join to E is designed to check for the absence of E in between
